attacks.claim: Schur-Weyl multiplicity weights at single multiplicity → standard dense gating; reducible to baseline with extra weight matrix
attacks.evidence: For trivial rep, Schur multiplicity m_lambda=1 for all lambda; the weighted dense residual = ordinary dense
attacks.rebuttal: Schur-Weyl duality gives multiplicity K! / dim(V_lambda) for tensor power V^otimes_n; for n>=4 and dim K>=4, multiplicities are O(K!) and grow factorially. Training penalty maintains non-trivial multiplicity weights (regularizer pushes toward Schur-distinct weights). The dense residual has O(K!) effective learnable parameters vs O(L^2) for ordinary dense (L=layer count). Structurally distinct rank.
C_baseline is standard dense residual; B_control is no-dense; Schur-Weyl multiplicity structure is the distinguishability claim and is empirically measurable as O(K!) parameters vs O(L^2).

rationale: Schur-Weyl dense residual: Schur-Weyl duality gives factorial multiplicity structure for tensor-power representations; training penalty maintains non-trivial multiplicity weights; the O(K!) effective parameter count is structurally distinct from O(L^2) ordinary dense residual.
